name: Portpal
description: See exactly what’s using your ports — and kill it in one click.
readme_summary: PortPal is a native desktop application designed to help developers manage and monitor network ports on their machines. It provides a real-time dashboard to view port usage, identify processes, track network traffic, and visualize network topology, enabling quick resolution of port conflicts and process management.
primary_use_case: Resolving and preventing 'address already in use' errors by providing developers with a clear, visual, and interactive way to see which processes are using specific network ports and to manage those processes.
cover_image_prompt: A digital conductor standing at a grand orchestra console, with each knob and slider representing a network port. The conductor is expertly adjusting the levels, with glowing lines of data flowing smoothly between different sections of the orchestra (representing connected services). Some 'instruments' (processes) are highlighted with a subtle red glow, indicating a conflict, while the conductor's hand is poised to resolve it with a single, decisive gesture. Small, stylized UI elements showing port numbers and process names are subtly integrated into the console. The overall mood is one of organized control and efficient problem-solving, rendered in a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration style with a vibrant, tech-inspired color palette.

name: SMS2Email
description: Android app that forwards SMS messages to SMTP (Email) server
readme_summary: SMS2Email is an Android application designed to forward incoming SMS messages to a specified email address. It allows users to access their text messages remotely by sending them to an SMTP server.
key_features: Forwards incoming SMS messages to email
primary_use_case: Enabling remote access to SMS messages by forwarding them to an email account.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, antique rotary telephone receiving a text message bubble. The text bubble is then magically transformed into an email envelope by a gentle stream of digital light. The scene is set on a clean, modern desk with subtle glowing circuit patterns on the surface. In the background, a small, unobtrusive screen displays a simplified email interface with incoming messages. The overall mood is one of seamless connectivity and accessibility, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a soft, warm color palette and clear, defined edges.

name: js-code-to-svg-flowchart
description: js2flowchart - a visualization library to convert any JavaScript code into beautiful SVG flowchart. Learn other’s code. Design your code. Refactor code. Document code. Explain code.
readme_summary: js2flowchart is a JavaScript library that converts JavaScript code into SVG flowcharts. It aids in understanding, documenting, and refactoring code by providing visual representations at various abstraction levels.
key_features: Converts JavaScript code to SVG flowcharts
Multiple abstraction levels for code visualization
CLI tool for generating SVG files
Flow tree modifiers for specific APIs (e.g., .map, .filter)
primary_use_case: Visualizing JavaScript code as flowcharts for learning, documentation, and refactoring.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing a chaotic library of scattered code snippets. With a magical quill, the librarian draws elegant, interconnected lines and shapes on a large scroll, transforming the jumbled code into a clear, beautiful SVG flowchart. The flowchart glows with organized information, while the scattered code snippets represent the input. Subtle digital particles and a small screen showing the 'js2flowchart' logo in the background connect the metaphor to software. The scene is set in a cozy, well-lit study with a warm color palette. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style with clean lines and a focus on clarity.

name: swagger-ui
description: Swagger UI is a collection of HTML, JavaScript, and CSS assets that dynamically generate beautiful documentation from a Swagger-compliant API.
readme_summary: Swagger UI is a JavaScript-based tool that generates interactive API documentation from OpenAPI specifications. It allows developers and consumers to visualize and interact with API resources without needing to implement the API logic, making API exploration and integration significantly easier.
key_features: Generates interactive API documentation from OpenAPI specifications
Allows visualization and interaction with API resources
Available as npm modules (swagger-ui, swagger-ui-dist, swagger-ui-react)
Can be served directly from a server or integrated into applications
primary_use_case: To automatically generate and display interactive API documentation from an OpenAPI specification, enabling easy exploration and testing of API endpoints.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized blueprint of a complex city, where each building represents an API endpoint. A friendly, glowing guide character, holding a magnifying glass and a tablet displaying code snippets, points to different buildings, illuminating their functions and connections. Small, interactive holographic displays hover around the guide, showing data flow and request/response examples. The overall scene is clean, modern, and inviting, with a clear focus on the guide and the illuminated API structures. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, optimistic color palette, subtly incorporating the 'Swagger UI' logo on the guide's tablet.

name: expo
description: An open-source framework for making universal native apps with React. Expo runs on Android, iOS, and the web.
readme_summary: Expo is an open-source framework for building universal native applications for Android, iOS, and the web using React and JavaScript. It provides a unified runtime and a suite of tools to simplify development, shipping, and iteration, integrating with Expo Application Services (EAS) for enhanced capabilities.
key_features: Universal native apps for Android, iOS, and web
Includes Expo SDK, Modules API, Go app, CLI, Router
Integration with Expo Application Services (EAS) for build, ship, and iterate
primary_use_case: Developing and deploying cross-platform native applications for mobile and web from a single codebase.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan standing at a central workbench, holding a single blueprint labeled 'Expo'. From this blueprint, three distinct, beautifully crafted objects emerge: a miniature smartphone (iOS), a miniature Android device, and a sleek laptop displaying a webpage. The artisan's hands are guiding streams of glowing code that flow from the blueprint to each device, seamlessly shaping them. Subtle digital particles and faint UI elements hover around the workbench, hinting at the underlying technology. The scene is bathed in a warm, inviting light, emphasizing creation and universality. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a vibrant, cohesive color palette.
